prompt: Recommend three works of fiction that you think every thoughtful adult should read at some point. Explain each in two or three sentences.
response:
1. One Hundred Years of Solitude by Gabriel García Márquez - This magical realist novel chronicles the multi-generational story of the Buendía family in the fictional Colombian town of Macondo. Through its dreamlike prose and metaphorical storytelling, it explores themes of isolation, fate, and the cyclical nature of history.

2. The Brothers Karamazov by Fyodor Dostoevsky - The last novel by the renowned Russian writer, this philosophical treatise follows the lives of the three Karamazov brothers and their father. It delves into deep questions about free will, faith, morality, and the inherent drama of the human condition.

3. To the Lighthouse by Virginia Woolf - A landmark work of modernist literature, this novel centers around the Ramsey family and their visits to their summer house in the Hebrides. Through its stream-of-consciousness narration and focus on the inner lives of its characters, it grapples with themes of mortality, legacy, and the search for meaning.
